title: ESBL Antibiotic Susceptibility Disc Diffusion Test
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"kjut","text":"To describe the procedures for the ESBL antibiotic susceptibility disc diffusion test.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"dtlv4","text":"Scope","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7as7a","text":"Extended-spectrum β-lactamase (ESBL)-producing gram-negative pathogens are a major cause of resistance to ESBL antibiotics. Since their discovery in the early 1980s, they have spread worldwide and are now endemic in Enterobacterales isolated from both hospital-associated and community-acquired infections. As a result, they are a global public health concern (Castanheira, 2021). Microbial resistance through ESBL has emerged globally, and ESBL-producing Enterobacteriacae are recognized worldwide as nosocomial pathogens of major importance. Several phenotypic tests have been recommended for screening and confirmation of ESBL-producing organisms, but these are usually performed on isolated organisms following culture and antibiotic susceptibility testing. (Abdelmoktader, 2019).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":456,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bd8fs","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ba5q4","text":"Disc (or Disk) diffusion by the Kirby-Bauer method is a standardized technique for testing rapidly growing pathogens. Briefly, a standardized inoculum (i.e., direct suspension of colonies to yield a standardized inoculum is acceptable) is swabbed onto the surface of Mueller Hinton (MH) agar. The inoculum must be standardized to the 0.5 McFarland Standard. Because reproducibility depends on the log growth phase of organisms, fresh subcultures are used. Filter paper disks impregnated with a standardized concentration of an antimicrobial agent are placed on the surface, and the size of the zone of inhibition around the disk is measured after overnight incubation. (Christenson, 2018).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8r953","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6qc2o","text":"Responsibility","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7up8u","text":"This SOP applies to all staff members and students. These individuals must be knowledgeable about the requirements set forth within this document. The lab manager or designee shall ensure that all staff and students know the proper techniques.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Incubate Overnight at the appropriate temperature for the isolate – usually 35 °C to 37 °C .
Aseptically choose a fresh overnight isolate with a sterile inoculating loop and inoculate into 2 mL of Mueller Hinton broth.
Prep:
Disc cartridges need to be at Room temperature , remove from 4 °C storage prior to starting the assay.
Make 2 mL aliquots of PBS in the 5 mL disposable Falcon tubes (or other clear tube) – one for each sample and one for the ‘blank’ control.
In the biosafety cabinet, air-dry two Mueller Hinton plates per sample and get to Room temperature .

Prep your bench area with appropriate pipette and tip volumes, kimwipes, ethanol, and racks for tubes. **NOTE: Depending on how many samples you have that day to process, only do a couple at a time. It will be very important to keep the samples cool to prevent further cell growth, so keep samples On ice or on the cool blocks or at 4 °C .
Turn on the Spectrophotometer to warm up. Depending on the machine, this can take up to 00:05:00 .
Gently mix, then transfer 1 mL overnight Mueller Hinton broth to a labeled, sterile 2 mL microcentrifuge tube.
Centrifuge for 5 x g, °C, 1 minute and discard the supernatant.
Add 100 µL PBS to the solid pellet left in tube and vortex.
Slowly add this suspension to 2 mL PBS to reach a 0.5 McFarland standard. This can be checked visually against the 0.5 McFarland standard on the Wickerham card.
When the 0.5 McFarland standard has been reached, fill a cuvette with the suspension, and check the suspension on the spectrophotometer.
Follow the settings for your specific machine but making sure it is set to read at 600 nm.
Place a ‘Blank’ cuvette (filled with PBS) into the Spectrophotometer, press “Measure Blank”.
Place first sample into machine, press “Measure Sample.” The reading should be between 0.08 – 0.13.
Continue with the rest of the samples. It is important to keep the samples cool to prevent further cell growth, so keep samples On ice or on the cool blocks or at 4 °C .
Antibiotic Disc Stamping
Discs must be at Room temperature . See Step 3.1 .
Label Mueller Hinton plates with the sample ID, date/initials, and with DDT (Double Disc Synergy Test) or CDT (Combination Disc Test).

Vortex the adjusted media. Swirl cotton swab around in the adjusted media, rub extra broth onto side of tube to get rid of excess drips. Swab entire plate with media, rotate plate 30⁰, swab entire plate again, rotate 30⁰ and repeat. Apply discs within 00:15:00 of inoculation.

Dip forceps in ethanol and flame sterilize. Once sterilized, use the forceps to aseptically place discs on the Mueller Hinton plate following the template in Appendix C go to step #23 . Sterilize the forceps again after use. ESBL Assay Type Antibiotic Discs 1 CDT CTX, CTX/CA, CAZ, CAZ/CA 2 DDT CTX, AMC/CA, CAZ
Incubate for 18±2 hours at 35⁰C±2⁰C Invert plates prior to incubation and incubate plates within 00:15:00 of disc application.
Using calipers (set to mm) or a ruler, measure the Zone of Inhibition (ZOI) around each of the antibiotic discs in mm. See Appendix B go to step #22 .
CDT results are considered positive for ESBL if there is ≥5 mm between the single drug and the drug plus clavulanic acid (CA). (See Appendix B go to step #22.4 ).
DDT results are considered positive if ZOI is larger on the AMC side for both CTX and CAZ halos. A bowtie or barbell shape indicates a negative test. (See Appendix B go to step #22.5 ).
Record the measurements on a data sheet for analysis.
Manual Zone Measurement using either a metric ruler or digital calipers.
Use the inoculum suspension optimally within 00:15:00 of preparation, and always within 01:00:00 . Keep samples cool to prevent further cell growth.
If the reading is off, start over with a new 2 mL PBS and slowly re-inoculate the suspension, then re-read on the spectrophotometer.
Place adjusted tubes in the refrigerator or keep On ice until next step.

Measure the diameter of the entire zone of inhibition (ZOI) in millimeters (mm), including the diameter of the disc. Each zone diameter should be clearly measurable; overlapping zones prevent accurate measurement. Hold the petri plate a few inches above a black background illuminated with reflected light. The zone margin should be considered the area showing no obvious visible growth that can be detected with an unaided eye. Ignore faint growth of tiny colonies that can only be detected with a magnifying lens at the edge of the ZOI.
If there are colonies within the ZOI, measure from the colony closest to the disc to the center of the disc (radius) and double that for the diameter.

What do the measurements mean? CDT results are considered positive for ESBL if there is ≥5 mm between the single drug and the drug plus clavulanic acid (CA). CDT Positive Examples CDT Negative Examples
If there is a double zone of inhibition (hazy zone around the clear zone), only measure the zone that is free from bacterial growth (completely clear).
Mueller Hinton Agar (MHA)

To make 1 Liter: Weigh 38 g Mueller Hinton Agar powder. Clean and turn off the scale. Add the 38g Mueller Hinton Agar powder to 1-Liter distilled water in a 2 L bottle. Heat while stirring and boil for 00:01:00 . Autoclave at 121 °C for 00:15:00 . Let cool to approximately 50 °C in a water bath. Pour 15 mL of the liquid agar into sterile petri dishes and let cool. Label the storage container and store at 4 °C .
Mueller Hinton Broth (MHB)

To make 1 Liter: Weigh 21 g Mueller Hinton Broth powder. Clean and turn off the scale. Add the 21g Mueller Hinton Broth powder to 1-Liter distilled water in a 2 L bottle. Heat while stirring and bring the mixture to a boil. Autoclave at 121 °C for 00:15:00 . Let the nutrient broth cool to Room temperature . Working in the Biosafety Hood, pipette 1 mL of the broth into sterile 2 mL microcentrifuge tubes. Label the storage container and store at 4 °C .
Phosphate Buffered Solution (1x PBS)

To make 2 Liters: Measure 1800 mL of distilled water and pour into a 2 L bottle. Measure 200 mL of 10X PBS. Pour the 200 mL of 10X PBS into the bottle with the 1800 mL of water. Invert to mix. Autoclave at 121 °C for 00:15:00 . Cool to Room temperature before use. Label the bottle, and store at 4 °C .

What do the measurements mean? DDT results are considered positive if ZOI is larger on the AMC side for both CTX and CAZ halos. A bowtie or barbell shape indicates a negative test. DDT Positive Examples DDT Negative Examples
creator: Justine C. Condon
